title: deferredRelease pre-allocates fixed 8 MB HD-sized buffer that throws on any image larger than 1920x1080
description: When deferredRelease=true, a fixed 1920×1080×4 = ~8 MB ArrayBuffer is allocated before the image dimensions are known (lines 1412-1415 in eventsProgressive; lines 1741-1744 in eventsOneShot). If the decoded frame is larger (e.g. 4K, 12 MP RAW), preparePixelsForEmit throws 'exceeds reusable buffer capacity' rather than resizing. The buffer is never grown; every session allocates the full 8 MB regardless of actual image size. For sub-HD images the 8 MB is a waste; for larger images the feature silently breaks at runtime.

title: takeAndWrap unconditionally copies pixel buffer even when the WASM buffer is already going to be freed in the same tick
description: In takeAndWrap (line 1467-1469), after retainBufferView returns a subarray view into the WASM heap, if applyRegionAndDownsample returns the original pointer unchanged (pixels.data === buf.data) the code immediately copies the whole frame via `new Uint8Array(buf.data)` before the retain is released. For the common final-only decode path with no region/downsample, this is a full per-frame pixel copy (~8-32 MB for 12 MP) that could be avoided by using takeBuffer directly instead of retainBufferView when region+downsample are both no-ops.

title: Unconditional console.log on every decodeTiledRegionRgba8 and decodeTileContainerRegion call with string interpolation
description: decodeTiledRegionRgba8 (line 972-977) and decodeTileContainerRegion (line 1175-1180) emit a multi-field console.log with .toFixed() string formatting on every call regardless of any debug flag. These functions are used in the gallery tile decode hot path. String interpolation with six .toFixed() calls and template literals creates GC pressure on every tile decode. Similarly the LibjxlEncoder constructor emits a console.log on every encode (line 1986) and after every encode completes (line 2298). The perceptualConstancyApplyBulk also logs on every call (line 3156).

title: new TextDecoder() instantiated inside parsePpmHeader loop for each token (same issue in pgo-train.mjs)
description: parsePpmHeader in both benchmark-pgo.mjs (line 194) and pgo-train.mjs (line 210) constructs a new TextDecoder() on every call to decode each PPM header token. PPM headers have 4 tokens, so 4 TextDecoder objects are created per file. This is called per-file per-rep in benchmark loops. A module-level shared TextDecoder instance would eliminate the repeated object construction.

title: scanForValidJpeg has O(n*m) worst-case: for every 0xFF byte it invokes findValidJpegEnd which re-walks up to the range end
description: scanForValidJpeg iterates every byte offset looking for 0xFF 0xD8. For each candidate SOI it calls findValidJpegEnd which walks forward up to `limit`. In a range full of 0xFF bytes (e.g. invalid/corrupted non-tile regions) this is O(range_size^2). For a typical JXTC non-tile range containing metadata boxes this is bounded but for adversarial or malformed inputs could be slow. The pattern is identical to a naive substring search without the Boyer-Moore or KMP shortcircuit.

category: redundant float multiplications in bilinearResize rgba16/rgbaf32 inner loop
title: bilinearResize rgba16/rgbaf32 paths recompute four bilinear weights per pixel instead of hoisting y-weight multiplication outside the x loop
description: The rgba8 path (line 2859-2889) correctly precomputes x-axis fixed-point weights as xtIs[] outside the y loop. The rgba16 and rgbaf32 paths (lines 2890-2952) compute w00/w01/w10/w11 from scratch for every pixel (dstW*dstH times). Since yt is constant per row, (1-xt)*(1-yt) can be rewritten as (1-yt) * xWeight[dx] where the y-fraction is hoisted per row, halving float multiplications. This affects 16-bit and float decode pipelines for large images.

category: excessive realloc doubling wastes memory on large encodes
title: EncodeRgbaWithMetadata output buffer doubles on every JXL_ENC_NEED_MORE_OUTPUT which can waste up to 2x the final file size
description: When JxlEncoderProcessOutput returns JXL_ENC_NEED_MORE_OUTPUT the C++ code doubles outbuf_cap and reallocates (lines 729, 892, 1077, 1303 across encode variants). For large images where the initial size estimate is significantly off, repeated doublings waste WASM heap: a 10 MB output starting at 1 MB estimate requires 4 doublings ending at 16 MB — 6 MB wasted. The WASM heap is limited on mobile browsers. Using JxlEncoderOutputProcessedBytes (available in libjxl 0.11) or a more conservative 1.5× growth factor would reduce peak allocation.

title: PGO benchmark infrastructure exists but is not wired to a CI performance regression gate
description: benchmark-pgo.mjs and the pgo-train/corpus scaffolding provide solid encode/decode benchmarks with baseline vs candidate comparison and a 2% threshold (compareEncodeBenchmarks). However there is no CI step that runs these benchmarks and fails the build when performance regresses beyond the threshold. The infrastructure is present but not enforced, meaning silent regressions can land undetected.
